metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify expand-contract sequencing for the `apply_failclosed` → `fail_mode`-parameterized refactor: declare the function's default parameter value, enumerate every call site that must be updated atomically, and provide a rollback path (e.g., keep `apply_failclosed` as a thin wrapper with `fail_mode='closed'` until all callers are migrated).
findings.finding: The `apply_failclosed` refactoring — changing from hardcoded-closed to a per-criterion `fail_mode` parameter that 'defaults open' for project invariants — does not specify whether the existing `code_review/detectors.py` call site is updated atomically (flag-day) or via an expand-contract sequence, nor what the function's own default parameter value will be. If the default shifts to 'open', any caller of `apply_failclosed` that does not pass an explicit `fail_mode` silently changes behavior from block to pass; no rollback path is given for this specific refactor.
findings.location: § DET-invariant scan scoping & fail-open / What exists vs. what we build (Net-new: 'Generalize the DET-scan consumer')
findings.suggested_fix: Keep `apply_failclosed(...)` as a backward-compatible wrapper that calls the new `apply_fail_mode(..., fail_mode='closed')` implementation; migrate `detectors.py` to call the new function with explicit `fail_mode` from the criterion's routing entry; deprecate and later remove the old wrapper. This makes the migration expand-contract with no silent default change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the adapter that maps a `{plan_text, criterion_id, expect}` eval fixture to the three-pass review kernel and produces an `eval_solver`-compatible output dict, so `rebar criteria eval <id>` can be built without redesigning `run_case`'s scaffolding.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `rebar criteria eval <id>` can be delivered by 'extending the closed solver dispatch' in `eval_solver.run_case`, but the existing solver's invocation model is structurally incompatible with how a project criterion is evaluated: the three existing branches each stand up a disposable git repo, seed rebar tickets/epics, then invoke an agentic op that reads that store. A project plan-review criterion is evaluated by running its prompt through the three-pass review kernel against the plan text — no disposable store, no ticket creation. No adapter layer between these two invocation models is specified, leaving a capability gap that is asserted rather than designed.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Per-criterion eval runner'; AC: `rebar criteria eval <id>`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new `run_criterion_case(criterion_id, case, *, runner)` function in `eval_solver` (or a new `criteria_eval_solver.py`) that: (1) loads the criterion descriptor via `effective_criteria`; (2) runs the plan text from `case['plan']` through the review kernel Pass-1 finder for that criterion; (3) returns a `{findings: [...]}` dict compatible with the existing `FIRE_EXPECTS`/`NOFIRE_EXPECTS` scorer vocabulary. This is a separate code path from `run_case`, not an extension of its if/elif chain.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the ef7e contradiction: either remove 'batch.extra_criteria' from the remediation AC and replace with 'ProductionBatchRunner runtime injection', or update the body to match the AC — pick one mechanism and make both consistent.
findings.finding: ef7e contains an intra-child contradiction between its remediation AC and its remediation body regarding the Pass-1 fan-out mechanism. The remediation AC states 'The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive batch.extra_criteria templated list' but the remediation body explicitly states 'the runner… appends ONLY the project.<name>-prefixed descriptors… to the finder's tier-split set… gates/plan-review.yaml and the immutable schema are UNTOUCHED' — i.e., NO new doc field. These two statements directly contradict each other in the same child story.
findings.location: child ef7e-c3ed-296b-4642, section 'Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism' vs 'Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the remediation AC to read: '[ ] The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses ProductionBatchRunner runtime injection (not a new YAML/schema field); an activated project criterion is appended to the tier-split finder set after assemble_context, deduped against built-ins, with no changes to gates/plan-review.yaml or workflow.v3.schema.json.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] ef7e's overlay schema definition must include fail_mode: open|closed as an optional routing field (even though the DET consumer is in 7f0d), so the schema does not need to be re-opened when 7f0d lands.
findings.finding: ef7e owns the overlay schema (criteria_routing.json) but defers all DET handling to 7f0d, yet 7f0d requires that the overlay schema already supports the fail_mode field in routing records. ef7e's ACs and schema documentation do not commit to including fail_mode in the overlay schema definition, creating a dependency gap: 7f0d's AC 'Per-criterion fail_mode honored' depends on ef7e having defined and validated fail_mode in the routing schema, but ef7e's scope explicitly excludes DET criteria ('Project exec: DET criteria are story #2').
findings.location: child ef7e-c3ed-296b-4642 (overlay schema scope) and child 7f0d-0cb6-16a7-4f26 (depends on story #1)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ef7e AC or scope: 'The criteria_routing.json overlay schema includes fail_mode: open|closed as an optional per-criterion routing field (defaulting to open); the field is defined in story #1 even though the DET consumer that uses it is in story #2 (7f0d).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `load_criteria()` (which resolves `repo_root` internally via `config.repo_root()` without a parameter) is made per-repo-safe so packaged-criteria descriptors don't leak across repos in the MCP server — either add `repo_root` as a parameter to `load_criteria` (and key the cache on it), or document that packaged-criteria descriptors are repo_root-independent (which requires removing the internal `config.repo_root()` call from `_descriptor_from_prompt`).
findings.finding: The plan's approach for `effective_criteria(repo_root)` discovery routes through `load_criteria` as a callsite to update, but `load_criteria()` is `@lru_cache(maxsize=1)` and resolves `repo_root` internally via `config.repo_root()` (not as a parameter). This means in a long-lived MCP server serving multiple repos, the cached `load_criteria()` result embeds the first repo's `repo_root` for packaged criteria descriptors. The plan proposes `effective_criteria(repo_root)` call `get_prompt(id, repo_root)` for project criteria, but says nothing about how the PACKAGED criteria descriptors (built by `load_criteria()`) interact with per-repo `repo_root` in a multi-repo context. If `load_criteria` stays as a `maxsize=1` cache that resolves `repo_root` internally, cross-repo descriptor contamination from packaged criteria remains.
findings.location: § Overlay schema — 'Discovery — `effective_criteria(repo_root)`'; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:139–151 (_descriptor_from_prompt resolves repo_root via config.repo_root()); src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:172 (load_criteria @lru_cache(maxsize=1))

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State clearly what `engine_b.scan` scans at plan-review time (whole repo? `file_impact` subset? something else?), why that scan is meaningful when there is no diff (what does a pattern match mean for a plan not yet implemented?), and whether a whole-repo scan at plan-review time is the intended behavior or whether DET invariants should be scoped to `file_impact` files only at plan time.
findings.finding: The plan states that a DET invariant at plan-review time 'checks the plan's declared intent and repo context' and that 'an invariant that inherently needs a diff is N/A at plan time (fail-open).' But `grounding.engine_b.scan` is a whole-repo content scan (it runs opengrep/ast-grep over the repository tree). Running a whole-repo DET scan for every activated project invariant at plan-review time has no specified scan target (the plan text is not the scan target; the whole repo is). The plan provides no rationale for WHY a whole-repo scan is the right approach at plan time (as opposed to, e.g., scanning only the declared `file_impact` files), no performance characterization, and no statement of what 'checking the plan's declared intent' actually means in terms of the scanner's input.
findings.location: § DET-invariant scan scoping & fail-open — 'A DET invariant runs as a whole-repo content scan via the shared `grounding.engine_b.scan`'; 'plan-review has no diff, so a DET invariant checks the plan's declared intent and repo context'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to ef7e (or a designated child) asserting that a blocking LLM criterion whose call errors or times out fails open (skip = PASS, never blocks the gate).
findings.finding: No child AC explicitly owns LLM fail-open behavior: when a blocking LLM criterion's call errors or times out, it must fail open (skip = PASS). The parent states this as a distinct design decision and AC6 calls it out explicitly ('An LLM-block criterion that errors fails open'). ef7e covers LLM criterion activation but its ACs say nothing about error/timeout handling. 7f0d covers DET fail_mode only. 5065 handles the unified layer post-b744 but defers to plan-review-first. No child has an AC asserting LLM timeout/error → fail-open.
findings.location: child ef7e-c3ed-296b-4642 (primary gap); also absent from 7f0d-0cb6-16a7-4f26 and 5065-0192-cc5e-4d01
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ef7e AC: '[ ] A project LLM criterion configured to block whose call errors or times out fails open (treated as abstain → skip = PASS); a test asserts the error path does not propagate as a block.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the complete round-trip sanitization protocol: specify who generates the sanitized key (e.g. `_sanitize_criterion_id`), confirm the gate YAML's `when` expressions reference the sanitized form, and add a load-time collision check after sanitization.
findings.finding: The `include_<id>` output-key sanitization rule (`.`→`_`) is stated in the plan but the protocol for who performs it, when, and how the batch `when` expression is generated to match is absent — leaving an implementer unable to build a collision-free, round-trip-consistent fan-out for project criterion ids.
findings.location: § Overlay schema, activation & caching — 'the `include_<id>` output key must sanitize the `project.<name>` id, e.g. `.`→`_`, to a valid workflow output key'; workflow_ops.py:154-165 (existing `include_{cid}` construction)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `_sanitize_cid(cid: str) -> str` helper (e.g. `cid.replace('.', '_').replace('-', '_')`) and use it in both `plan_review_assemble_criteria` (output key) and the gate YAML template generator (when expression). Add a load-time assertion that no two criterion ids produce the same sanitized key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either specify the async job+poll endpoint contract (submit path, job-ID scheme, poll path, terminal response shape) or explicitly mark the async fallback as out-of-scope for this epic (with a follow-on ticket), so the executor has a complete or bounded spec.
findings.finding: The editor live-preview spike-gate fallback to 'an async job+poll shape' is referenced but its contract (job-submission endpoint path, job-ID scheme, poll endpoint, poll response shape, client-side timeout/retry) is entirely unspecified, leaving a full endpoint design to the executor.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `POST /criterion/preview` async fallback BEFORE committing to the endpoint design: either (a) name the specific fallback mechanism (e.g. background thread + poll URL with a `job_id`, citing how shared state is managed in `ThreadingHTTPServer`), (b) cite a prior-art pattern for async job+poll in Python stdlib HTTP servers, or (c) decide upfront to ship only the synchronous shape (with a hard timeout returning a timeout error to the editor, not a silent hang) and defer async as a follow-on if the spike proves it necessary.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that POST /criterion/preview can 'fall back to an async job+poll shape rather than blocking the editor's ThreadingHTTPServer handler' without specifying what that shape is, citing a precedent for it in Python stdlib, or naming the mechanism — making this an unverified capability assertion gated only on a runtime spike rather than a design-time resolution.
findings.location: § 'What exists vs. what we build' — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' bullet: 'Spike gate: if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an async job+poll shape rather than blocking the editor's ThreadingHTTPServer handler'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decompose into at least 3 child tickets: (A) registry/overlay/attestation/DET/LLM core (build-order steps 1–2), (B) eval CLI + calibration view (step 3), (C) editor live-preview (step 4) — and treat post-b744 unification (step 5) as a separate follow-on ticket explicitly gated on b744 landing.
findings.finding: The plan is epic-scale (8 ACs, 5 build phases, spanning registry, overlay schema, attestation, DET consumer, eval tooling, editor live-preview, and cross-gate unification) with a structural 'and' joining multiple independent subgoals, but no formal child tickets are defined. The editor live-preview (a new synchronous HTTP endpoint with a spike-gate for async fallback) and the post-b744 code-review unification are independently completable and independently risky, yet they appear as phases within one plan rather than separate children.
findings.location: § Build order, § What exists vs. what we build (net-new), Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Move AC #7 (code-review adoption of `rebar.llm.criteria`) out of this epic's acceptance criteria into a tracked follow-on item (e.g., a child ticket or a clearly labelled 'post-b744' AC section), so the current epic has a clean, in-session-completable AC set.
findings.finding: AC #7 (shared `rebar.llm.criteria` layer consumed by both plan-review and code-review) is listed as an acceptance criterion for this epic but is explicitly gated on `b744` landing, making it not in-session completable within this epic's scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #7: 'A shared `rebar.llm.criteria` layer that both plan-review and code-review consume…'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC #7 in this epic with a narrower, in-session-verifiable AC: 'The `rebar.llm.criteria` shared layer is implemented and consumed by plan-review; a documented migration path for code-review adoption post-b744 is recorded in the ADR.' Move the full unification verification to the b744 follow-on.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact field lookup chain for `scenario` in the DET fallback descriptor: name the precise key(s) to check (e.g. `envelope.get('description') or rule.get('message') or id`) and confirm whether these fields need to be added to the `Detector` envelope schema.
findings.finding: The DET fallback descriptor's `scenario` field is defined as 'the detector rule's `message`/description from its `.rebar/detectors/<id>` envelope', but the `Detector.envelope` dataclass (grounding/detectors/registry.py) has no `message` field — the decision rule (which field name takes precedence, where it lives, and the fallback when neither is present) is stated in the plan section but not co-located with the schema it governs.
findings.location: § Overlay schema — 'DET fallback descriptor shape … scenario = the detector rule's `message`/description from its `.rebar/detectors/<id>` envelope'; grounding/detectors/registry.py — `Detector` dataclass and `envelope: dict[str, Any]`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fully specify the `POST /criterion/preview` response schema: enumerate the required/optional fields of `finding` (or declare it a single finding dict with required keys), specify what `rationale` contains for DET criteria (e.g. the matched snippet + rule id), and clarify the `finding` field presence rule on `no-fire`.
findings.finding: The `POST /criterion/preview` response schema references `verdict: "fire"|"no-fire"` and `finding?: <findings.py finding shape>` but the value vocabulary for `finding` is undefined: it is unclear whether `finding` is a single finding dict or a list, which fields of the findings shape are required vs optional for this context, and how DET criteria (which produce no LLM rationale) populate the `rationale` field.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'POST /criterion/preview … → {verdict: "fire"|"no-fire", finding?: <findings.py finding shape>, rationale: str}'

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the spike-gate fallback behavior to a verifiable AC (e.g., 'if preview exceeds timeout, the server returns a 202+job-id immediately and exposes a poll endpoint') and include it explicitly in Build Order step 4, or explicitly mark it as a follow-on / out-of-scope with rationale.
findings.finding: The editor live-preview 'spike gate' fallback (async job+poll shape) is described in the Net-new section but is absent from both the Acceptance Criteria and the Build Order, leaving it uncovered by any verifiable AC and unscheduled in the implementation sequence.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — Net-new, editor live-preview bullet: 'Spike gate: if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an async job+poll shape...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the docs deliverable an explicit commitment to update (not just supplement) existing CLAUDE.md `.rebar/` extension-point sections to cross-reference the new activation requirement, ensuring 'presence != active' is visible wherever `.rebar/detectors/`, `.rebar/prompts/`, and `.rebar/evals/` are documented.
findings.finding: Existing CLAUDE.md documentation for `.rebar/` extension points (`.rebar/prompts/`, `.rebar/detectors/`, `.rebar/<gate>_moves.json`) will become misleading after the overlay is introduced, because the plan only adds a new 'project-supplied criteria' section without committing to updating those existing subsections to note that activation now requires an explicit `activate` entry in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`. A user reading the existing `.rebar/detectors/` docs would believe adding a file is sufficient to activate a project invariant.
findings.location: § Migration & compatibility — Docs deliverable / What exists vs. what we build — 'Project extension points'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) drop the spike-gate condition and specify only the synchronous shape (threads handle blocking correctly in `ThreadingHTTPServer`), or (b) fully specify the async fallback: job-id response schema, poll endpoint path + response schema, TTL, cleanup, and token guard.
findings.finding: The `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint's async fallback shape ('fall back to an async job+poll shape') is named as a spike-gate alternative but is entirely underspecified: no job-id response schema, no poll endpoint path, no result schema, no job TTL or cleanup, and no error shape when the async job itself fails — leaving an implementer with no executable protocol for the non-trivial case.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview … Spike gate: if a single sync preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an async job+poll shape rather than blocking the editor's `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler'
findings.suggested_fix: Given that `ThreadingHTTPServer` spawns one thread per request, a 60s blocking handler does not starve other requests — the simplest correct fix is to remove the spike-gate clause and specify only the sync shape, adding a note that the 60s timeout causes a delayed 200/timeout response on that thread only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Include `rebar criteria eval <id>` CLI reference documentation (flag list, output format specification for recall/false-accept/κ/agreement, exit codes) as an explicit deliverable alongside the ADR and CLAUDE.md how-to.
findings.finding: The new user-facing CLI command `rebar criteria eval <id>` is not listed in the documentation deliverables; only an ADR and a CLAUDE.md how-to are specified. A new CLI subcommand with calibration output (recall, false-accept, κ, agreement %) constitutes a new contract that needs a reference doc or at minimum a --help specification included in the deliverable scope.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build (Net-new: 'Per-criterion eval runner') / Success Criteria / AC: 'rebar criteria eval <id>'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify ADR ownership: specify that ef7e writes the initial ADR 0015 (plan-review overlay/activation/cache contract) and 5065 extends it with the cross-gate unification section, OR designate exactly one child as the sole ADR author.
findings.finding: Both ef7e and 5065 independently claim to produce the ADR for the unified registry. ef7e's remediation section states 'docs/adr/0015-project-supplied-criteria.md documents: the overlay schema, merge+collision/rebind rules, effective_criteria/effective_routing as the vocabulary seam, the content-hash cache-isolation contract…' and ef7e AC6 says 'An ADR documents the unified-registry + overlay merge/activation contract.' 5065 AC7 says 'An ADR + CLAUDE.md/docs/ how-to documents the unified registry across gates.' Both children claim ADR authorship without establishing which is the authoritative source or that one extends the other.
findings.location: child ef7e-c3ed-296b-4642 (AC6, remediation ADR section) and child 5065-0192-cc5e-4d01 (AC7)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ef7e scope: 'Writes docs/adr/0015-project-supplied-criteria.md covering plan-review overlay, activation, cache isolation, and the vocabulary seam contract (stub sections for code-review marked TODO until 5065 lands).' Add to 5065 scope: 'Extends ADR 0015 with the cross-gate unification, posture divergence, and code-review attestation sections; adds CLAUDE.md how-to.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete adoption-level validation mechanism to the Success Criteria — e.g., a dogfooding story (rebar's own `.rebar/` activates a project criterion end-to-end as part of CI), or an operational metric (fraction of must-fire fixtures caught ≥ N% on the reference fixture set for a pilot project).
findings.finding: No concrete adoption-level validation mechanism exists for the named beneficiary (project maintainers). All ACs are functional correctness gates; there is no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric (e.g., fraction of project-specific violations caught that were previously missed), or dogfooding story that would confirm the feature actually solves the adoption problem.
findings.location: Success Criteria + Acceptance Criteria sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Success Criteria: 'The rebar repo itself activates at least one project criterion (DET + LLM) in its own `.rebar/` overlay and passes its CI gate — serving as the dogfooding pilot and the concrete before/after validation.' This costs nothing (the MVP slice already builds the machinery) and gives a binary, observable adoption signal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide between uncached and `(repo_root, overlay mtime)`-keyed caching for `effective_routing(repo_root)` and state the chosen strategy explicitly in the overlay schema section.
findings.finding: The caching strategy for `effective_routing(repo_root)` is stated as two unresolved alternatives ('uncached, or keyed by (repo_root, overlay mtime)'), leaving the executor to choose an approach rather than implementing a decided design.
findings.location: § Overlay schema, activation & caching — 'Caching (clears G6 cache bug)' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Choose `(repo_root, overlay mtime)` keying (stat the overlay file, use mtime as cache key component) — it handles the MCP server long-lived process case without per-call overhead and the RED test can assert that changing the overlay file mtime busts the cache.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'Routing derivation/validation pipeline' is in-scope (add a covering AC with verifiable done-ness criteria) or out-of-scope (move it to an Out-of-scope note and remove from the Net-new list).
findings.finding: The 'Routing derivation/validation pipeline' is listed as a Net-new deliverable ('What exists vs. what we build') but has zero covering Acceptance Criteria, making it an orphaned described deliverable. The parenthetical '(criteria_routing.json is hand-maintained; no generator/drift gate)' is ambiguous — it could mean this pipeline IS to be built (replacing hand-maintenance) or merely documents current state as out-of-scope. An implementer cannot determine scope or done-ness.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — Net-new list, last bullet: 'Routing derivation/validation pipeline'
findings.suggested_fix: If in scope: add an AC such as 'A `rebar criteria validate-routing` command (or CI check) detects drift between declared criteria ids and the overlay routing entries, exits non-zero on mismatch.' If out of scope: move to the Out-of-scope section with rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion for the editor live-preview endpoint covering: the `POST /criterion/preview` response shape (`verdict`, `finding?`, `rationale`), the configurable timeout (default 60s), and the async fallback condition — so the editor authoring extension has an end-state-observable proof.
findings.finding: The editor live-preview endpoint (`POST /criterion/preview`) is described in detail as a net-new deliverable (endpoint shape, timeout, spike gate, fallback to async) and included in Build Order step 4, but there is no corresponding Acceptance Criterion to make it testably complete.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview'; § Build order step 4; Acceptance Criteria (no matching AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] `POST /criterion/preview` accepts `{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture}` and returns `{verdict: "fire"|"no-fire", finding?: <finding shape>, rationale: str}` within the configured timeout (default 60s); if the synchronous call does not return within the timeout, the endpoint falls back to an async job+poll response rather than blocking the `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism by which a newly activated project criterion enters the Pass-1 batch fan-out in plan-review.yaml (e.g. YAML regeneration via a `rebar workflow generate` step gated in CI, or replacing the static batch list with a dynamic op that reads `effective_criteria` at runtime and dispatches dynamically); the current plan leaves the static YAML unchanged, which makes the feature inoperative.
findings.finding: The plan states that 'project ids must enter [the workflow Pass-1 batch vocabulary]' and that 'workflow_ops builds the `include_<ID>` fan-out from the canonical set at workflow-definition time' — but the actual plan-review.yaml is a STATIC file with every criterion's `when: ${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_<ID> }}` hardcoded. `plan_review_assemble_criteria` does emit `include_project_foo` outputs for project criteria (since it builds from `effective_criteria`), but there is NO corresponding batch entry in the static YAML to consume that output — so the activated criterion's `include_project_foo=True` is silently unused and the criterion never fans out. The plan does not specify how the static YAML is extended (regenerated? replaced with a dynamic batch step?).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the static per-criterion `when:` batch list in plan-review.yaml with a single dynamic batch step driven by `effective_criteria(repo_root)` at runtime — the batch runner already iterates its criterion list, so making that list data-driven (returned by the assemble op) avoids any YAML regeneration. Alternatively, if YAML regeneration is preferred, define a `rebar criteria sync` command that rewrites the YAML and add it as a CI drift gate.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
